title: planchonella novozelandica
intro: Planchonella novozelandica, also known as the New Zealand cherry, is a species of tree native to New Zealand and surrounding islands. It is valued for its timber and medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic. The plant's bark and leaves are used to make herbal preparations that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and skin infections, and are also believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits associated with Planchonella novozelandica include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as promoting wound healing and overall well-being.
